note: Materialized python-package catalog: package name -> serving backends + offload-able entries. For Pattern A (cpython-ext static linkage), the worker IS the composed python.wasm forge artifact (a single worker serves many entries) — env = sha256(python.composed.wasm). For native-tier offload, env = sha256 of the composectl-composed v86 worker artifact. composectl handles composition/exec; this catalog is the single python-facing source of truth a driver consults before building a plan.

entries.doc: Compress bytes to zlib format (RFC 1950) via the DEFLATE codec.
Decompress zlib (RFC 1950) or raw DEFLATE (negative wbits) bytes.

source.shim: cpython-ext/_compression/zlib.py (installed as Lib/zlib.py at forge-build time)
source.note: Backend env is sha256(python.composed.wasm) for the current browser forge build. The same artifact also serves hashlib, ssl, sqlite3, and xxhash entries — Pattern A means the interpreter is the worker. When the python.composed.wasm artifact is rebuilt (new wasi-sdk, new cap, new CPython), this env shifts and the entry must be re-registered. Add a v86-tier backend by digesting the v86 variant's python.composed.wasm when built.
